search.body: Use media as evidence and usable content rather than as interchangeable atmosphere. Verify the primary subject is recognizable and materially inspectable in the first relevant viewport. Decorative overlays can hide detail and weaken trust. Atmospheric media may lead a narrative experience when detailed inspection is not the user goal.

search.title: Per-Section Posterization
search.summary: Every long-page section resets into another hero with giant display type, excessive lead-in space, outline words, numbered labels, or a full visual spectacle.
search.body: Preserve one display crescendo and create section hierarchy through content, media, composition, density, and interaction instead of repeated headline scale. Use hero-scale typography at one display apex and at most one intentional secondary climax. Step ordinary section headings down materially and remove empty bands that exist only to frame type. Rewrite adjacent sections that repeat the same heading, spacing, and grid move. Changing background color or alignment does not make repeated hero sections structurally different. An explicit poster, exhibition, or typographic narrative may use repeated display moments when the sequence itself is the concept and viewport fit remains intact.

search.title: House-Style Convergence
search.summary: Unrelated briefs repeatedly converge on the same macrostructure and motif bundle, such as dark canvas, fluorescent accent, mono section labels, hard grids, terminal chrome, and giant condensed type.
search.body: Keep UI Forge product-derived by separating subject matter from style and rotating complete direction systems instead of recoloring a familiar shell. Name three tempting genre defaults before selection and use at most one without brand, asset, or reference evidence. Compare against existing project surfaces or recent artifacts when available; reroute if the macrostructure and two or more strong motifs repeat. Reject unsupported bundles such as condensed ultra-bold type, uppercase mono labels, fluorescent ticker, saturated artifact panel, offset shadow, and hard rules when three or more recur together. Each motif may be valid alone; the unsupported recurring combination is the failure. An established brand system or explicit reference may intentionally own the recurring combination.

search.title: Primary Workflow Complete
search.summary: The requested core path works from its initial state through a truthful completion, recovery, or explicitly bounded endpoint.
search.body: Prevent visually polished shells from being delivered while the main user outcome remains placeholder or broken. Execute the primary path and verify every reachable control has a real outcome. A prototype limitation must be visible and reported rather than presented as complete. A user requesting only a static design artifact may not require runtime behavior.

search.title: Semantic Keyboard Path
search.summary: Native semantics, programmatic names, logical focus order, and keyboard operation cover the complete core workflow.
search.body: Ensure the interface remains operable and understandable without a pointer or visual inference alone. Navigate the primary path with keyboard only and inspect names, roles, values, and focus order. Clickable containers and custom widgets often omit required keyboard behavior. Purely decorative noninteractive content has no keyboard path.

search.title: Visible and Unobscured Focus
search.summary: Every keyboard-focusable element has a visible high-contrast indicator that sticky or modal UI does not hide.
search.body: Keep keyboard location evident throughout navigation, disclosures, dialogs, and dense control regions. Inspect focus indicators across light, dark, selected, error, and sticky-layer contexts. Browser-default focus may become invisible after custom borders or overflow clipping. Noninteractive elements should not receive focus merely to satisfy this gate.

search.title: Contrast and Non-color Cues
search.summary: Text, meaningful graphics, controls, focus, and states meet relevant contrast thresholds and do not depend on color alone.
search.body: Preserve legibility and state meaning across vision differences, displays, and environmental conditions. Measure every semantic color pairing used and inspect state cues without relying on hue. Review hover, active, focus-visible, selected, disabled, error, and success states through source, design-state inspection, or manual rendered review when practical. Token names do not prove the actual foreground and background pairing passes. A foreground that passes on the default surface may fail when a state changes only the background. Logotypes and incidental disabled content follow the specific WCAG exceptions.

search.title: Responsive Without Loss
search.summary: Supported widths and text zoom preserve content, controls, reading order, and recovery without clipping or incoherent overlap.
search.body: Make narrow screens and zoomed text first-class design conditions rather than a visual afterthought. Inspect at 320 CSS pixels, representative mobile and desktop widths, and 200 percent text zoom. Hiding overflow can conceal a defect instead of fixing it. Inherently two-dimensional canvases and tables may use bounded scrolling with accessible controls.

search.title: Honest Content
search.summary: People, data, customer evidence, metrics, integrations, awards, and product claims are source-backed or clearly labeled as samples.
search.body: Prevent generated UI content from misrepresenting product facts or creating false trust evidence. Trace each factual claim and evidence-like asset to a source or sample label. Plausible placeholder content can be mistaken for real data during release. Clearly labeled fictional fixtures are valid in demos and automated tests.

search.title: Recoverable Errors
search.summary: Real failure paths explain what happened, preserve useful input, and provide a clear retry, correction, undo, or escape route.
search.body: Keep users in control when validation, network, permission, or destructive actions fail. Trigger each material failure state and complete its recovery path. Generic error toasts without context or next action do not satisfy recovery. A purely static page with no failure path does not need artificial error UI.

search.title: Reduced Motion Remains Functional
search.summary: Reduced-motion preference removes nonessential travel and autoplay while preserving state feedback and every user outcome.
search.body: Avoid vestibular triggers and ensure motion is never required to understand or operate the interface. Enable reduced motion, execute the primary path, and verify feedback remains visible. Disabling all transitions without replacement can hide state changes. Motion essential to the information requires a documented accessible alternative where possible.

search.title: Stable Layout
search.summary: Media, loading, validation, long labels, and async results reserve space and do not unexpectedly move controls or content.
search.body: Prevent layout shifts, missed actions, and overlapping UI across normal state transitions. Compare geometry across loading, longest-content, error, success, and empty states. Rigid heights can trade layout shift for clipping; use responsive constraints. Content regions may grow naturally when growth does not displace an active control unexpectedly.

search.title: Readable Measure and Wrap
search.summary: Headings, body copy, metadata, and actions retain intentional line lengths without brittle hard breaks or accidental one-word columns.
search.body: Prevent responsive typography from looking cut apart when display type, side padding, or a narrow max-width consumes the available measure. Inspect the longest real heading, paragraph, label, and action immediately before and after every layout breakpoint. Remove ordinary-copy hard breaks and reject measures that repeatedly produce one- or two-word body lines when more width is available. A layout can avoid overflow and still be unreadable because padding or type scale leaves an unusably narrow text column. A short editorial phrase may use a deliberate fixed break when it remains coherent at every supported width and zoom level.

search.title: First Viewport Fit
search.summary: A hero-led entry fits its required identity, orientation, proof, and primary action within the declared short-viewport budget without clipping or crowding.
search.body: Prevent oversized display type, fixed hero heights, and excessive spacing from pushing required landing-page content outside the visible first screen. At default text size, inspect the longest real entry at a representative short desktop and short mobile height after fixed chrome and safe areas. Record the visible viewport height, fixed-chrome bottom, required-content bottom, hero boundary, and first meaningful next-section cue; reject clipping, overlap, hidden actions, empty-background reveals, or a title that crowds out required proof. At text zoom, allow natural vertical growth and verify every item remains reachable rather than preserving the one-screen composition. Width-only screenshots miss vertical failures caused by short laptops, mobile browser chrome, and accidental title wrapping. Do not force a pass by shrinking body text or controls below readable sizes, transform-scaling the hero, or hiding overflow. An explicitly requested poster or immersive title screen may spend more than one viewport on its entry when required actions and content remain reachable and unclipped.

search.title: Container and Bleed Separation
search.summary: Viewport-wide backgrounds and media extend independently while text, controls, and repeated panels remain aligned to the shared content grid.
search.body: Prevent wide-screen whitespace, double-applied gutters, and misaligned sections caused by mixing viewport calculations with constrained child panels. Compare the computed x position and width of adjacent constrained sections at wide desktop, compact desktop, tablet, and mobile sizes. Verify full-bleed paint reaches the intended viewport edges while document scroll width remains equal to viewport width. Using a viewport gutter formula inside an already centered max-width child applies the outside spacing twice. A deliberate offset or asymmetric editorial field is valid when its misalignment is specified and remains responsive.

search.title: Reference Transformation
search.summary: Studied references inform principles and craft without reproducing one source's recognizable shell, navigation, typography, and accent composition.
search.body: Keep reference-led work specific to the target product and prevent a redesign from reading as another product with substituted copy. Name the traits retained from each reference and identify at least two structural decisions derived from the target product. Compare thumbnail silhouettes and redesign when the shell, navigation geometry, hero structure, and accent placement still match one source as a set. Changing colors and copy does not create a distinct system when the page silhouette and chrome remain recognizable. Direct reproduction is valid only when the user owns the source and explicitly requests faithful implementation.

search.title: Design-system Coherence
search.summary: Typography, spacing, color, icons, shape, surfaces, and interaction states follow one deliberate foundation.
search.body: Prevent visual and behavioral drift caused by arbitrary values and mixed component systems. Scan the implementation for duplicate roles, arbitrary tokens, and mixed icon or component systems. For repeated metrics, labels, icons, and controls, declare the intended center, edge, baseline, or decimal alignment and inspect rendered geometry. Consistency should not erase necessary hierarchy or state differences. A migration boundary may temporarily host two systems with explicit isolation.

search.title: Authentic Primary Media
search.summary: Primary imagery reveals the actual subject users need to inspect and includes correct crop, dimensions, loading, and alternative text.
search.body: Make media useful evidence rather than generic atmosphere or an unstable decorative layer. Inspect the subject, crop, loading behavior, reserved space, and text alternative at representative widths. Dark overlays and aggressive crops often hide the subject and reduce contrast. Abstract or atmospheric imagery is valid when detailed inspection is not a user need.

search.title: Verified Delivery Report
search.summary: The final report names executed checks, inspected viewports and states, material fixes, and any remaining limitations.
search.body: Give reviewers a factual account of confidence instead of a generic claim that quality checks passed. Separate UI/UX review, static inspection, existing project checks, rendered observations, automated evidence, and untested risk. Do not imply that a command, viewport, browser, or e2e test was inspected when it was not. A conceptual-only response should state that no implementation checks apply.

search.title: SaaS and Operations
search.summary: Quiet, repeatable workspaces for frequent tasks, scanning, comparison, triage, and stateful business workflows.
search.body: Prioritize speed, predictability, data clarity, and comprehensive states over decorative marketing composition. Use stable navigation and aligned controls for repeated actions. Expose loading, empty, permission, error, success, and stale states. Keep headings and chrome compact enough to preserve working space. Avoid oversized heroes, decorative card walls, and expressive motion that slows repeated work. Onboarding and empty states may use more editorial spacing while preserving clear actions.

search.title: Commerce and Product Detail
search.summary: Inspectable product media paired with essential configuration, trust, fulfillment, and purchase information.
search.body: Help users evaluate and act without hiding the product behind atmosphere or scattering key facts across decorative sections. Show the real product clearly in the first viewport. Keep price, availability, configuration, fulfillment, returns, and primary action legible. Preserve selected variants and validation through errors. Do not fabricate reviews, scarcity, ratings, or customer activity. Luxury editorial commerce may use more narrative pacing when purchase facts remain accessible.

search.title: Editorial and Portfolio
search.summary: Authored reading rhythm, distinctive composition, and high-quality media for stories, essays, work, and case studies.
search.body: Balance expressive identity with legible reading, truthful attribution, and robust narrow-screen ordering. Keep text measure, heading hierarchy, and captions readable. Use media crops and pacing that serve the narrative. Retain authorship, dates, credits, and navigation context. Expressive layout must not scramble DOM order or hide links and captions. Experimental art experiences may prioritize exploration when the user explicitly requests it.

search.title: Data and Analytics
search.summary: Decision-oriented views that preserve comparison, units, filters, freshness, provenance, and drill-down context.
search.body: Choose tables and charts from user questions instead of decorating dashboards with arbitrary metrics. State the decision or question each visualization supports. Show units, time range, filters, freshness, and no-data states. Provide labels and non-color distinctions with a table or text alternative where needed. Do not use 3D charts, truncated axes, or metric cards without decision context. A monitoring wall may optimize distant scanning over detailed interaction.

search.title: Mobile Application
search.summary: Platform-aware navigation, reachable actions, safe-area handling, keyboard resilience, and explicit gesture alternatives.
search.body: Design for touch, limited height, interruption, and platform conventions rather than compressing a desktop interface. Account for safe areas, software keyboard, and short landscape height. Keep primary controls reachable and targets appropriately sized. Provide explicit alternatives to drag and swipe outcomes. Desktop hover, dense multicolumn layouts, and browser-style chrome rarely transfer directly. Tablet layouts may introduce secondary columns when content and input mode support them.

search.title: Campaign and Brand
search.summary: Expressive first viewports that establish the literal offer or subject and reveal evidence of the next section.
search.body: Create brand impact without generic split heroes, abstract decoration, hidden subjects, or unsupported claims. Make the brand, offer, place, person, product, or object a first-viewport signal. Use authentic or clearly identified media that reveals the subject. Keep the next content band partially visible on representative screens. Do not put the hero message inside a floating card or use a generic text-media split by default. A restrained institutional campaign may prioritize trust and clarity over expressive composition.

search.title: Next.js App Router
search.summary: Use Server Components by default, narrow client boundaries, framework routing and metadata, and explicit static-generation contracts.
search.body: Keep UI implementations aligned with current Next.js architecture while avoiding hydration, rendering, and deployment mistakes. Keep interactive Client Components small and pass serializable data into them. Use framework layouts, links, metadata, loading, error, and not-found conventions. For static export, generate every dynamic path and avoid unsupported server features. Do not access browser globals during server rendering or add client boundaries to entire route trees without need. A heavily interactive editor may justify a larger client-owned island with explicit boundaries.

search.title: React Component System
search.summary: Model state explicitly, preserve semantic elements, keep component ownership local, and avoid unsafe HTML or unnecessary effects.
search.body: Produce maintainable UI behavior without over-abstracted components, duplicated state, or unsafe rendering escape hatches. Use native controls and controlled state only where the workflow needs it. Keep effects for synchronization with external systems, not derived render data. Render untrusted content as text or sanitized data, never directly through unsafe HTML. Generic components with many boolean props often hide unclear ownership and state combinations. A vetted rich-text pipeline may render sanitized HTML through a tightly controlled boundary.

search.title: Tailwind Utility CSS
search.summary: Use a shared token vocabulary, stable responsive rules, and extracted components only where repetition creates real ownership.
search.body: Keep utility-first styling coherent without arbitrary-value drift, unreadable class duplication, or premature wrapper components. Map recurring semantic colors, spacing, radii, and type roles to shared tokens. Keep responsive behavior near the component and verify class ordering remains readable. Extract repeated component structure, not one-off class lists. Frequent arbitrary values and duplicated state selectors indicate a missing token or primitive. A truly unique fixed-format element may require one explicit arbitrary constraint.

search.title: Plain HTML and CSS
search.summary: Prefer semantic document structure, progressive enhancement, modern layout primitives, and minimal JavaScript for stateful behavior.
search.body: Build durable interfaces without recreating browser controls or adding a framework where the product does not need one. Use landmarks, native controls, form labels, and logical heading structure. Use grid, flexbox, container constraints, and media queries for layout. Make content and core navigation useful before optional JavaScript enhancements. Custom select, dialog, tab, and menu implementations carry substantial accessibility requirements. A complex application may require a state framework once interaction ownership exceeds local enhancement.

search.title: Native Mobile UI
search.summary: Use platform components, navigation, safe-area behavior, dynamic type, accessibility APIs, and native feedback conventions.
search.body: Keep mobile interfaces familiar, reachable, and resilient across text sizes, input methods, and device geometry. Prefer platform controls and accessibility semantics before custom drawing. Test dynamic text, keyboard, safe areas, rotation, and screen-reader order. Use native navigation and feedback behavior unless the brand requirement clearly outweighs it. Web hover and breakpoint assumptions do not map directly to native application layout. Games and immersive media may use custom-rendered controls with equivalent accessibility paths.

search.title: Three.js Interactive 3D
search.summary: Use a proven 3D engine, stable full-bleed canvas geometry, explicit loading and fallback states, and measured input performance.
search.body: Make 3D the actual experience rather than a decorative preview while keeping the page usable when rendering or assets fail. Verify nonblank rendered pixels and correct framing at mobile and desktop sizes. Provide loading, error, reduced-motion, and non-WebGL fallback behavior. Keep keyboard, touch, pointer, resize, and visibility lifecycle behavior coherent. Do not place the primary 3D experience inside a decorative card or omit rendered framing checks when a render is practical. A small 3D inspector within an operational tool may use a bounded functional viewport.
